sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 65.2g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 65.2g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 13.7g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 13.7g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 15.2g. essential amino acids score is where things start to fall off. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 15.2g. essential amino acids score is where things start to fall off. protein is support here, not the whole argument.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 4% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 4% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 38% DV, zinc at 21% DV, iron at 16% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 38% DV, zinc at 21% DV, iron at 16% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. contains useful polyphenols because adds plant-compound value beyond the core table. has strong healthy-fat branding appeal because a practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats. can be very satiating because dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

displayItems.title: contains useful polyphenols
displayItems.explanation: Adds extra plant-compound value beyond the core table.

displayItems.title: has strong healthy-fat branding appeal
displayItems.explanation: A practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats.

displayItems.title: can be very satiating
displayItems.explanation: Dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side. contains useful polyphenols because adds plant-compound value beyond the core table. has strong healthy-fat branding appeal because a practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats. can be very satiating because dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. easy to overeat calories quickly because energy density adds up fast. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can go rancid if stored badly because a practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

displayItems.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

displayItems.title: can go rancid if stored badly
displayItems.explanation: A practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

sections.subtitleText: the drawbacks next. easy to overeat calories quickly because energy density adds up fast. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can go rancid if stored badly because a practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

closing.summary: Walnuts has a genuinely strong overall case, especially contains useful polyphenols and has strong healthy-fat branding appeal. the downsides are there, but easy to overeat calories quickly and contains some anti-nutrients do not outweigh the upside. best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 65 from a base nutrition score of 65. Context barely changed the result.
